cartridge.name: Magic Desk Plus
cartridge.description: Commodore 64 cartridge format with up to 1MB ROM, 128KB battery-backed SRAM, and 8KB or 32KB EEPROM. Supports bank-switching and page-switching for reading and writing persistent memory.
cartridge.developer_notes: SRAM and EEPROM share the same page-selection register and mapping window, selected mutually exclusively via a control register.

portions.description: Second 64KB SRAM portion (contains the initialization signature)

io_window.description: Access window for the currently selected page of SRAM or EEPROM.

DE00.name: ROM_BANK_REG
DE00.description: Classic Magic Desk ROM bank switching register. Writing a bank number maps the corresponding 8KB ROM bank to the $8000-$9FFF memory range.

DE01.name: PAGE_REG
DE01.description: Selects the active 256-byte page of SRAM or EEPROM mapped to the $DF00-$DFFF window.

sram.description: Selects page 0 to 255 of the currently active 64KB SRAM portion.

eeprom_8kb.description: Selects page 0 to 31. Bits 5-7 are ignored.

eeprom_32kb.description: Selects page 0 to 127. Bit 7 is ignored.

DE03.name: CTRL_REG
DE03.description: Control register for RAM/EEPROM mapping. Since this register is write-only, a shadow copy should be kept in system RAM to track and modify its state.

bit_fields.name: SRAM_BANK_SELECT
bit_fields.description: Selects the active 64KB portion of SRAM.

bit_fields.name: MEMORY_SELECT
bit_fields.description: Toggles between EEPROM and SRAM access.

values.0: SRAM disabled, EEPROM enabled (Default at power-on)

signatures.purpose: Used to detect hardware presence and verify if the non-volatile memory has been initialized by software.

sram.description: The last two bytes of the 128KB SRAM.

eeprom_8kb.description: The last two bytes of the 8KB EEPROM.

eeprom_32kb.description: The last two bytes of the 32KB EEPROM.

steps: 1. Save current shadow register value of $DE03.
2. Enable SRAM and select SRAM portion 1 (set Bit 5 and Bit 0 of $DE03 shadow register, write to $DE03).
4. Read bytes at offsets 254 ($FE) and 255 ($FF) of the $DF00-$DFFF window.
5. If read values match the signature (0xCA, 0xF1), SRAM is PRESENT and INITIALIZED.
6. If values do not match: test SRAM functionality by saving current values, writing 0xCA and 0xF1, and reading them back.
7. If values read back match the signature, SRAM is PRESENT and UNINITIALIZED. Restore original saved values.
8. If values do not match, SRAM is ABSENT or non-functional.
9. Restore the saved shadow register value to $DE03.
1. Save current shadow register value of $DE03.
2. Enable EEPROM (clear Bit 5 of $DE03 shadow register, write to $DE03).
3. Check for 32KB EEPROM initialized signature: select page 127, read offsets 254 and 255. If matched, it is 32KB PRESENT and INITIALIZED. Skip to step 9.
4. Check for 8KB EEPROM initialized signature: select page 31, read offsets 254 and 255. If matched, it is 8KB PRESENT and INITIALIZED. Skip to step 9.

5. Test 32KB EEPROM write functionality: write 0xCA and 0xF1 at page 127, offsets 254 and 255 (using the write-delay). Read back. If matched, 32KB EEPROM is PRESENT and UNINITIALIZED. Restore original values and skip to step 9.

6. Test 8KB EEPROM write functionality: write 0xCA and 0xF1 at page 31, offsets 254 and 255 (using the write-delay). Read back. If matched, 8KB EEPROM is PRESENT and UNINITIALIZED. Restore original values and skip to step 9.
7. If all tests fail, EEPROM is ABSENT or non-functional.
8. Restore the saved shadow register value to $DE03.

eeprom_programming.description: Standard EEPROMs (e.g., AT28C64 or AT28C256) require a programming delay of up to 10 milliseconds per write operation. When programming, the software must insert a delay loop after writing a byte to the $DF00-$DFFF window before changing the page, switching banks, or writing another byte.

sram_enable: mdp_ctrl_shadow |= 0x20; // Set bit 5 to enable SRAM
sram_disable: mdp_ctrl_shadow &= ~0x20; // Clear bit 5 to disable SRAM (enables EEPROM)
eeprom_write_byte: void mdp_eeprom_write_byte(uint8_t page, uint8_t offset, uint8_t value) {
// Timing delay for EEPROM cell write verification

sram_enable: ; Enables SRAM and switches to the second 64KB portion
ora #$21             ; Set Bit 5 (SRAM enable) and Bit 0 (Portion 1)
eeprom_write: ; Write value to EEPROM page $12, offset $34
